new_text: Shodh-Memory vs Torus mem2x (Session 394). SCORES: Pre-mem2x 48.0; mem2x 58.4; Shodh 80.5. CATEGORY (Pre→mem2x→Shodh): Retrieval 48→59→83; Storage 58→67→74; Learning 11→29→76; Speed 55→53→92; Token 79→80→77; Reliability 75→73→85; Output 65→73→76; Multi-Agent 64→65→75. MEM2X ADDED: hybrid decay (exp→power-law, 15-day crossover); 4-level LTP (1.0/0.5/0.33/0.1); knowledge graph (SQLite, Hebbian, spreading activation); 6-pass entity extraction. GAPS vs Shodh: memory replay (0 vs 80); pattern detection (0 vs 75); adaptive weights (0 vs 80); implicit feedback (5 vs 90); neural NER (45 vs 85); flat edges vs L1→L2→L3; no concept merging; Python 200-800ms vs Rust 34-60ms. BEST COMBO: Combo 2 (session-boundary replay+cluster detection+retrieval-outcome weights): 58.4→68.3, ~500 LOC, Low-Med risk.

Memory-system audit (Session ~263+): Gate 4/boot.py/memory chain. FINDINGS: 1. Boot pre-satisfies Gate 4: boot_pkg/orchestrator.py L406 calls _write_sideband_timestamp() unconditionally at SessionStart → 5-min free pass (Edit) or 10-min (Write). 2. Memory injection is display-only: inject_memories_via_socket() (boot_pkg/memory.py:29-89) queries ChromaDB via UDS socket with generic query — not session-aware, no filtering by project/tags. 3. Gate 4 sideband file: ~/.claude/hooks/.memory_last_queried, atomic write, checked by enforcer. 4. ChromaDB socket at /tmp/memory_server.sock (UDS). 5. Boot always runs before first tool call → boot pre-querying memory counts as satisfying Gate 4 for initial window. Risk: agents could bypass memory discipline by relying on boot injection alone.

Solana Architecture (Feb 2026). RUNTIME: Sealevel parallel execution; txs declare accounts upfront, non-conflicting run in parallel. Accounts: 5 fields (lamports, data, owner, executable, rent_epoch); max 10MB; only System Program creates. Programs: sBPF via LLVM; Anchor vs Pinocchio (perf). Anchor types: Signer, Account, Program, SystemAccount, UncheckedAccount, InterfaceAccount. Constraints: init, mut, seeds+bump, has_one, close, realloc. PDAs: deterministic off-curve keys (seeds+bump). TOKENS: Mint + Token account per owner; ATA = PDA(owner, mint). Token-2022: transfer fees, confidential transfers, interest-bearing. DEFI: AMM x*y=k; orderbooks rare on-chain. Fees: base 5000 lamports + compute units. Compute: 200k default, 1.4M max. Slot: ~400ms; epoch: ~2 days.

SuperClaude Framework (Session 296). Meta-programming config for Claude Code; NOT Anthropic-affiliated. 30 /sc: commands; 16 agents; 7 modes; 8 MCP servers; Python pkg 3,002 lines + pytest plugin. MCP servers: context7, sequential-thinking, magic, playwright, morphllm-fast-apply, serena, tavily, chrome-devtools. ENFORCEMENT: no gate system; SelfCheckProtocol (4 Questions, 94% hallucination detection); ConfidenceChecker 0-1.0 (>=90% proceed, 70-89% investigate, <70% research); ReflexionPattern 70%. TOKEN: 8k->3.2k (60%); tasks 43k->27k (37%). PERF: 3.5x faster via parallel waves. TESTS: 100% pass; 95.26% coverage. VERSION: v4.2.0 (2026-01-18). Git: master<-integration<-feature. UV only.

Claude-Mem (thedotmack/claude-mem) token usage analysis. SESSION START: injects up to 50 observations (CLAUDE_MEM_CONTEXT_OBSERVATIONS=50) + 10 session summaries via SessionStart hook. Each observation ~50-100 tokens; 5 most recent get full display with narrative field. Includes header, legend, context index, context economics, timeline, footer. Total: 2,000-5,000+ tokens/session start. ARCHITECTURE: SQLite-backed; observations table (PostToolUse auto-capture); summaries table (LLM-generated at Stop). HOOKS: SessionStart (inject), PostToolUse (capture), Stop (summarize). KEY DIFFERENCE from Torus: flat SQLite vs LanceDB vector; no semantic search; pure recency injection; no gate integration; simpler but higher context cost.

Claude Code notification + terminal title research (2026-03-10). BACKGROUND TASK NOTIFICATIONS: run_in_background=true → completion message injected into conversation: 'Background command completed (exit code 0)'. Mechanism: process exit monitoring + event injection into conversation context. NO separate escape sequence or OS signal. TERMINAL TITLE: Claude Code sets terminal title via OSC escape sequences during active work; resets to shell default on exit. NOTIFICATION BELLS: sends terminal bell on task completion if terminal supports it. IDLE STATE: after run_in_background completion, Claude reactivates from idle. No configurable notification hooks exposed to users. Behavior is internal to Claude Code runtime — not customizable via settings.json or hooks.

Claude Code ConfigChange Hook (2026-02-23). INTRODUCED: v2.1.49 (not 2.1.45). Fires when config files change mid-session. TRIGGERS (source field values): user_settings→~/.claude/settings.json; project_settings→.claude/settings.json; local_settings→.claude/settings.local.json; env_settings→.env file changes. PAYLOAD: {event: ConfigChange, source: ..., path: ..., changes: {}}. TIMEOUT: 3s default. BLOCKING: exits 2 blocks the change (rolls back). TORUS IMPLEMENTATION (commit 8878228): handler config_change.py; matcher 'user_settings|project_settings'; 3s timeout. USE CASES: enterprise security auditing; preventing unauthorized settings changes; logging config drift. NOT triggered by: CLAUDE.md edits; memory_server config; gate file changes (only settings.json variants).

Commit 8878228: Adopt 3 Claude Code features (spinnerTips, ConfigChange, keybindings). CHANGES: 1. settings.json L196-207: ConfigChange event hook; matcher 'user_settings|project_settings'; handler config_change.py; 3s timeout. 2. spinnerTips array (L253-264): 10 tips covering memory-first, read-before-edit, testing, causal chain, gate awareness, quality-over-speed. 3. Keybindings: custom shortcuts added. config_change.py: logs changes to audit trail, warns on critical field modifications (model overrides, gate disables), blocks if tamper detected. SpinnerTips visible during Claude Code loading spinner — used for framework best-practice reinforcement. All 3 features native Claude Code capabilities, no external deps.

Torus Gate Inventory (2026-03-09). TIER 3: G17 INJECTION; G18 CANARY; G19 HINDSIGHT. DORMANT: G08, G12.

Pyright import analysis for test_framework.py (2026-02-24). ROOT CAUSE: Pyright cannot follow runtime sys.path.insert() calls. test_framework.py uses sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(__file__)) at L15 to put hooks/ on sys.path for bare imports; Pyright is static. TWO IMPORT CATEGORIES: 1. shared.* imports work — shared/ has __init__.py and hooks/ is root. 2. Direct gate imports (import gate_01_read_before_edit) fail Pyright — no package structure. SOLUTION OPTIONS: pyrightconfig.json extraPaths=['hooks']; per-file type:ignore; pyproject.toml pythonpath. CHOSEN: pyrightconfig.json extraPaths=['hooks']. Does not affect runtime behavior. Gate files remain importable. Zero false positives after fix.

Audit of ~/.claude/hooks/shared/ (2026-02-16). DEAD CODE: 1. audit_log.py log_event (L151): backward-compat alias for log_gate_decision, never used, can remove. 2. chromadb_socket.py ping() (L93): only used in test_framework.py, never in prod — keep for testing. 3. secrets_filter.py SecretsScrubber class (L65): stateless class wrapper, module uses module-level instance _scrubber, class never imported/instantiated elsewhere — internal detail, keep. SAFE TO REMOVE: log_event alias only. FINDINGS: 66 modules total; no other dead exports identified; backward-compat functions should remain unless explicitly deprecated. Recommendation: remove log_event in next cleanup pass; mark ping() as test-only; document _scrubber pattern.

Meteora DLMM. ID: LBUZKhRxPF3XUpBCjp4YzTKgLccjZhTSDM9YuVaPwxo (closed source). ARCHITECTURE: discrete price BINS (not tick-based CLMM). Price: (1 + binStep/10000)^binId. Bins grouped into BinArray accounts (70-100 bins); index = floor(binId/100). ACTIVE BIN: ONE active bin at market price; holds both tokens; earns ALL fees. Bins below = X only; above = Y only. POSITION: span of bins, liquidity shares per BinArray. Fees: baseFactor + variableFactor (volatility-scaled); fee bps = baseFactor x binStep. STRATEGIES: Spot (uniform); Curve (center); Bid-Ask (spread ends). TYPES: Permissionless; Permission (whitelist). ACCOUNTS: LbPair (pool), BinArray (liquidity), Position. DLMM vs CLMM: discrete prices, volatility fee.

Option C Memory Upgrade Plan (Session 392). DECISION: Build Shodh-inspired features into memory_server.py. Score: 82/100 vs 74/100. BRANCH: mem2x (worktree). COMPONENTS: 1. Hybrid Decay: exp days 1-3 (lambda=0.693); power-law day 3+ (beta=0.5/0.3); lazy on read. 2. Hebbian: co-retrieval -> boost connection. 3. LTP: None->Burst(5+/24h,2x)->Weekly(3+/wk,3x)->Full(10+,10x). 4. Entity Extraction (MEDIUM-HIGH): POS+suffix, compound nouns, Porter2, co-occurrence edges. 5. Three-Tier Flow: working(7)->session(100)->long-term; promote on 3+ accesses. 6. Knowledge Graph (2-3wk): SQLite+LanceDB, entity nodes+edges. 7. Spreading Activation: BFS+hop-decay, 3-6 hops. TOTAL: 3-5 weeks.

Dual-Layer Memory Architecture Survey (2025-2026). CORE: Episodic: events, append-only, deterministic retrieval, cheap write. Semantic: distilled facts, LLM write, k-NN+BM25. PAPERS: HiMem (2601.06377): immutable episodes + Note Memory; LOCOMO 80.71% vs A-MEM 51.88%. Synapse (2601.02744): unified directed graph; F1 40.5 (+7.2 vs A-MEM); 814 tokens/query. SimpleMem (2601.02553): entropy gating tau=0.35; consolidation tau=0.85; 30x token reduction; -31.3% multi-hop without consolidation. Mem0 (2504.19413): Extract-then-Update; +26% vs OpenAI memory; 90% token reduction. LANCEDB IMPL: episodes (append-only, TTL 30d) + semantic (async consolidation, confidence decay). Retrieval: episodic+semantic parallel, ~3K budget, freshest episodic wins.

M2A (arxiv 2602.07624): Multimodal Memory Agent with Dual-Layer Hybrid Memory. Feb 7, 2026. ARCHITECTURE: ChatAgent (ReAct: Query->Generate->Update) + MemoryManager (read-write only). Layer 1 RawMessageStore: append-only, immutable multimodal log. Layer 2 SemanticMemoryStore: compressed knowledge entries (text desc, auto image captions, associated images, evidence_ids). Three index vectors: dense text + sparse BM25 + cross-modal image. Tri-path retrieval fused via RRF (k=60). BENCHMARKS (LoCoMo, GPT-4o-mini): M2A 44.64% vs A-MEM 36.26%, Mem0 34.73%, LoCoMo RAG 33.27%. Qwen3-VL-8B: M2A 54.69% vs 43.95% (+23%); GLM-4.6V-Flash: 56.48% vs 47.46% (+19%). ABLATION: no dual-layer -13.31pp; no iterative retrieval -16.02pp; no tri-path -4.10pp. DATASET: 10 convs, ~621 turns, 214 images.

Session 11 (2026-02-10): Causal Fix Tracking -- 5 features, 7 files. 1. error_normalizer.py (NEW, 43 lines): strips paths/UUIDs/hex/timestamps; FNV-1a->8-char fingerprint; normalize_error(), fnv1a_hash(), error_signature(). 2. fix_outcomes ChromaDB + 3 MCP tools: record_attempt(error,strategy)->chain_id; record_outcome(chain_id,outcome)->Laplace (s+1)/(n+2), auto-ban if attempts>=2 AND confidence<0.18; query_fix_history->semantic+hash+30-day decay. 3. gate_09_strategy_ban.py (NEW, 42 lines): Tier 2, blocks Edit/Write on banned strategy. 4-5. state.py: 4 new fields (pending_chain_ids, current_strategy_id, current_error_signature, active_bans); MAX_ACTIVE_BANS=50, MAX_PENDING_CHAINS=10. TESTS: 174/175 passing. Chain IDs: {error_hash}_{strategy_hash}.

Memory pipeline analysis (Session 169). WRITE (remember_this): degraded check -> metadata cap 500 -> noise rejection (20 char min + 14 regex) -> near-dedup (cosine: 0.10 hard; 0.03 type:fix; 0.15 soft-dupe) -> citation extraction -> SHA-256[:16] ID -> ChromaDB upsert (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384-dim, cosine HNSW) -> FTS5 dual-write (BM25 + tag table) -> tag co-occurrence dirty flag -> sideband timestamp (.memory_last_queried) -> hybrid linking. READ (search_knowledge): query embed -> vector (top-20) + FTS5 (top-20) + tag expansion -> co-occurrence boost -> RRF merge -> L2/L0/L3 enrichment -> salience re-score -> project boost -> top-k. KEY NUMBERS: 0.10 hard dedup; 20-char noise floor; 14 noise patterns; 30-day decay half-life; 384-dim embeddings; HNSW cosine.

ActMem + M2A research (2026-03-11). ACTMEM (arxiv 2603.00026): retrieval-reasoning gap -- NaiveRAG 84.86% retrieval but 61.54% QA. Architecture: unified causal+semantic KG; 4 modules: fact extraction -> clustering (cosine tau=0.2) -> KG (semantic edges cos>0.3 + causal PMI tau=0.8) -> two-pass counterfactual retrieval (vector search -> LLM generates what-would-follow -> second retrieval). ~10pp improvement over standard RAG on multi-hop QA. M2A CORRECTION: actual improvement over best baseline is +23% (Qwen3-VL-8B) and +19% (GLM), NOT 40%. IMPLICATION FOR TORUS: counterfactual retrieval worth adding to search_knowledge; PMI-based causal edges complement Hebbian co-retrieval.
